content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a computational implementation of the educational concept of reflexive learning, where the 'dynamic memory' from the original Reflexion agent framework mimics the iterative vocabulary and critical thinking reflection practice taught in high school literacy curricula. Just as students deepen their language comprehension by repeatedly reflecting on word usage and contextual meaning across readings, LLMs can improve their reasoning by reflecting on inconsistencies between their own multiple problem-solving outputs, aligned with the Self-Contrast reflection approach.
